---
title: "Lucas's Website"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

<style type="text/css">
.title {
  display: none;
}

#getting-started img {
  margin-right: 10px;
}

</style>

<div class="row" style="padding-top: 30px;">
<div class="col-sm-6">

# **Lucas Nathe**

This is a working space for my personal website. I am currently an associate Data Scientist at the J.P. Morgan Chase Institute and a masters student studying computer science with a specialization in Artificial Intelligence at the Georgia Institute of Technology.


## What I've been working on lately..
1. I recently published a [report](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/institute/all-topics/financial-health-wealth-creation/new-income-driven-repayment-plan) exploring the potential effects of recent changes in Income-Driven repayment plans on federal student loans.
2. In an attempt to rebuff my Python, webscraping, and geocoding skills, I attempted to explore the determinants of Fringe Bank location in my hometown of Chicago, starting with proximity to a traditional bank in this [Jupyter Notebook](https://github.com/lmnathe/Chicago_FringeBanks/blob/main/Chicago_FringeBanks.ipynb).
3. Plotting a few google trend search results by DMA against the number of COVID-19 cases by month. [https://lmnathe.shinyapps.io/gtrendsapp/]. Note, the shinyApp currently takes about 30 seconds to launch.
4. A clean function to pull in CRA disclosure files county loan totals.. [https://github.com/lmnathe/read_cra]

</div>
<div class="col-sm-6">

<a href = "https://github.com/lmnathe/LucasNathe">
![](images/chase_headshot.jpeg)
</a>
</div>
Disclaimer: The views expressed on this site are mine alone and do not reflect the views of the J.P. Morgan Chase Institute or its staff.
